items.title: VI Live: Arsenal op jacht naar volgende ronde FA Cup, Celtic ontsnapt
items.summary: Zondag staan er vier wedstrijden in de Eredivisie op het programma. Het duel van Feyenoord volgen we in een aparte live. In deze VI Live blijf je op de hoogte van SC Heerenveen tegen PEC Zwolle, Telstar tegen FC Twente, Sparta Rotterdam tegen NEC én het belangrijkste buitenlandse voetbal.

items.title: Arsenal v Wigan Athletic: FA Cup fourth round – live
items.summary: ⚽ FA Cup news from the 4.30pm GMT kick-off in London ⚽ Live scoreboard | Follow us on Bluesky | And mail John Myles Lewis-Skelly in midfield is the move a few Gunners fans have been waiting for. He’s a left-back when in the first team but found it tough to win a place. He’s been bred as a midfielder. Touch of the Trent Alexander-Arnold there, perhaps. Kevin Archer gets in touch: “I may be over-tired and easily confused, but did you say in your opening preamble that the FA Cup was “a holiday for Ryan Lowe and his team”, and then go on to inform us that Ryan Lowe had been sacked last week? Not much of a holiday that. Reminds me of the time I went on holiday recently and both my kids came down with chicken pox. Super.” Continue reading...

items.title: Duel tussen Sparta en NEC definitief gestaakt vanwege sneeuw
items.summary: De wedstrijd tussen Sparta Rotterdam en NEC Nijmegen moet op een later moment worden gespeeld. Het Kasteel werd zondagmiddag geteisterd door zware sneeuwval, waardoor de arbitrage een streep moest zetten door het duel.

items.title: Sunderland heeft heldenrol Roefs ditmaal niet nodig voor vijfde ronde FA Cup
items.summary: Weinig verrassingen in de FA Cup zondagmiddag. Alle Premier League-clubs bekerden verder ten koste van Championship-tegenstanders. Voor Sunderland is het de eerste keer sinds elf jaar dat het de vijfde ronde van de FA Cup bereikt.

items.title: Wolves show guts to edge past battling Grimsby and into FA Cup fifth round
items.summary: Santiago Bueno’s second-half winner ensured a safe passage into the FA Cup fifth round for Wolves as the League Two side Grimsby bowed out with pride intact. The Premier League visitors had not won away in any competition since last April and look certain to be relegated to the Championship this season. But Rob Edwards’s side showed impressive resilience to avoid the kind of ignominy which led to Manchester United being dumped out of the Carabao Cup here this season. Continue reading...

items.title: 'I'm buzzing' - Man Utd's Skinner toasts seventh-straight win
items.summary: Manchester United are getting the job done when it matters most - and their seven-game winning streak has gone under the radar.

items.title: Diarra's controversial penalty earns Sunderland FA Cup win at Oxford Utd
items.summary: Sunderland reached the FA Cup fifth round for the first time since 2015 as Habib Diarra's contentious first-half penalty earned a 1-0 win away to Oxford United.

items.title: Fraaie comeback brengt Heerenveen zege op PEC Zwolle
items.summary: SC Heerenveen heeft dankzij een knappe comeback PEC Zwolle verslagen. De Friezen kwamen zondag razendsnel met 0-2 achter, maar nadat Maxence Rivera uit het niets de aansluitingstreffer maakte, nam de thuisclub het duel in handen, om die niet meer weg te geven.

items.title: Tengstedt was verrast dat Van Persie tegen Utrecht geen beroep op hem deed
items.summary: Casper Tengstedt werd zondag de matchwinner bij Feyenoord door een strafschop in blessuretijd binnen te schieten. De Deense spits is allesbehalve zeker van zijn plek, maar mede door blessures kreeg hij ditmaal een halve wedstrijd de kans van Robin van Persie. Vorige week bleef hij nog negentig minuten op de bank, ondanks dat Ayase Ueda ontbrak. En dat had hij ook zelf niet verwacht.

items.title: Bueno the hero as Wolves beat valiant Grimsby to earn FA Cup fifth round spot
items.summary: Santiago Bueno scored and blocked a sure goal in stoppage time to secure Wolves a spot in the FA Cup fifth round in a narrow 1-0 win at a valiant Grimsby.

items.title: Perri makes key save as Leeds sink Birmingham in FA Cup shootout
items.summary: Leeds advanced to the fifth round of the FA Cup, with sights set on a possible first quarter-final place since 2003. But after scoring a last-gasp equaliser to take the tie to extra time and then a penalty shootout, Birmingham could draw some consolation from pushing their top-flight opponents. Sean Longstaff scored the clinching penalty, after Patrick Roberts, who had scored in the 89th minute to earn Birmingham parity, blasted his over the crossbar. Lucas Perri also saved from Tommy Doyle, who was probably man of the match. Continue reading...

items.title: Real Madrid 'lucky to have' Trent, says Arbeloa
items.summary: Alvaro Arbeloa praised Trent Alexander-Arnold, saying Real Madrid are "lucky to have" him after the full-back starred, providing an assist, in Real Madrid's 4-1 win over Real Sociedad.

items.title: Macclesfield's Rooney: 'Ambition' to manage higher
items.summary: John Rooney is clear in his ambition to manage at the highest level possible but he wants to go on that journey with Macclesfield.

items.title: Barça's Flick praises ref 'good communication'
items.summary: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said his chat with the referee from Thursday's 4-0 defeat to Atlético Madrid is a secret, but praised the communication.

items.title: Leeds survive FA Cup scare, win penalty shootout
items.summary: Leeds United survived in the FA Cup to win a penalty shootout against Birmingham City on Sunday.

items.title: Reijnders warns Arsenal: Man City 'on the hunt'
items.summary: Tijjani Reijnders has warned Arsenal that Manchester City remain firmly "on the hunt" for the Premier League title.

items.title: Chelsea 2-0 Liverpool, Aston Villa 3-7 Tottenham and more: WSL clockwatch – as it happened
items.summary: Spurs put seven past Aston Villa in 10-goal thriller while Lauren James stars as Chelsea beat Liverpool What a devastating counter attack from Everton! They have an overload going forward and work it to Hannah Blundell. Blundell plays the ball into the path of Honoka Hayashi who taps into an empty net. Well this is a surprise. London City Lionesses work it well down the right and deliver the ball into the box. Nikita Parris bravely meets it and heads the ball back across goal. What a start for the away side. Continue reading...

items.title: GB's Bankes, Nightingale win Winter Olympics gold in snowboard cross
items.summary: Great Britain have claimed a second gold medal at the 2026 Winter Olympics after Charlotte Bankes and Huw Nightingale won the mixed team snowboard cross event.

items.title: Reed hits winner as Fulham hit back to dump Stoke out
items.summary: Harrison Reed's opportunistic finish carried Fulham into the fifth round of the FA Cup as they came from behind to beat Stoke 2-1 at the bet365 Stadium.

items.title: Alexander-Arnold assists opener as Real Madrid thrash Sociedad
items.summary: Trent Alexander-Arnold claims an assist on his first start in more than two months as Real Madrid beat Real Sociedad to move top of La Liga.
